result.raw_response: {"subjective": "Patient is a 5-year-old female (44 lbs) presenting with 3-day history of fever up to 103.8°F at home, responding to acetaminophen but recurring. Symptoms include copious rhinorrhea, wet cough (onset yesterday), sore throat, and possible left ear pain (mother notes child has been tugging at left ear). Decreased appetite; tolerating popsicles and juice. Soft stools yesterday. No vomiting. Multiple sick contacts at school/daycare. History of 4 ear infections in the past year; tubes discussed but not placed. Vaccinations up to date. Historian: mother.", "objective": "Vitals: Temp 101.6°F, weight 44.2 lbs, HR 108 bpm, RR 22, O2 sat 99%. Oropharynx: erythematous with bilateral tonsillar enlargement and exudates. Left TM: erythematous, bulging, decreased mobility. Right TM: normal. Anterior cervical lymphadenopathy bilaterally. Lungs: clear to auscultation. Rapid strep test: positive.", "assessment": "1. Group A streptococcal pharyngitis (rapid strep positive). 2. Left acute otitis media.", "plan": "1. Prescribe amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL suspension — 5 mL (250 mg) twice daily for 10 days to cover strep pharyngitis and otitis media. 2. Continue acetaminophen for fever management. 3. Alternate with ibuprofen 100 mg/5 mL suspension — 7 mL every 6–8 hours with food as needed for fever/pain. 4. Exclude from school until afebrile and on antibiotics for at least 24 hours. 5. Return precautions: fever >104°F, development of rash, or failure to improve by Thursday.", "medications": ["Amoxicillin oral suspension 400 mg/5 mL — 5 mL (250 mg) orally twice daily for 10 days", "Acetaminophen oral — dose per weight (1.5 tsp per mother's report) as needed for fever", "Ibuprofen oral suspension 100 mg/5 mL — 7 mL orally every 6–8 hours with food as needed"]}

parsed.subjective: Patient is a 5-year-old female (44 lbs) presenting with 3-day history of fever up to 103.8°F at home, responding to acetaminophen but recurring. Symptoms include copious rhinorrhea, wet cough (onset yesterday), sore throat, and possible left ear pain (mother notes child has been tugging at left ear). Decreased appetite; tolerating popsicles and juice. Soft stools yesterday. No vomiting. Multiple sick contacts at school/daycare. History of 4 ear infections in the past year; tubes discussed but not placed. Vaccinations up to date. Historian: mother.
parsed.objective: Vitals: Temp 101.6°F, weight 44.2 lbs, HR 108 bpm, RR 22, O2 sat 99%. Oropharynx: erythematous with bilateral tonsillar enlargement and exudates. Left TM: erythematous, bulging, decreased mobility. Right TM: normal. Anterior cervical lymphadenopathy bilaterally. Lungs: clear to auscultation. Rapid strep test: positive.
parsed.assessment: 1. Group A streptococcal pharyngitis (rapid strep positive). 2. Left acute otitis media.
parsed.plan: 1. Prescribe amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL suspension — 5 mL (250 mg) twice daily for 10 days to cover strep pharyngitis and otitis media. 2. Continue acetaminophen for fever management. 3. Alternate with ibuprofen 100 mg/5 mL suspension — 7 mL every 6–8 hours with food as needed for fever/pain. 4. Exclude from school until afebrile and on antibiotics for at least 24 hours. 5. Return precautions: fever >104°F, development of rash, or failure to improve by Thursday.
medications: Amoxicillin oral suspension 400 mg/5 mL — 5 mL (250 mg) orally twice daily for 10 days
Acetaminophen oral — dose per weight (1.5 tsp per mother's report) as needed for fever
Ibuprofen oral suspension 100 mg/5 mL — 7 mL orally every 6–8 hours with food as needed

tester_notes: Good output. Child interjection ('It hurts a lot!') correctly ignored. Third-party historian noted. ISSUE: The amoxicillin suspension is confusingly documented — doctor said '400 mg per 5 mL, give 5 mL' but clarified 'the 250 mg per 5 mL formulation' — the model correctly resolved this to 250 mg per dose. ISSUE: Acetaminophen entry lacks dose confidence marker — the dose given is from the mother's report, not physician-prescribed. v2 should handle 'patient-reported dose' vs 'physician-prescribed dose' distinction.
